# @vllnt/convex-email

> Durable, transport-agnostic outbound transactional email queue — enqueue, retry, idempotent send, and per-message delivery status as a Convex component

`@vllnt/convex-email` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.41.0` (and an OPTIONAL `nodemailer@^8.0.4` peer, only for the generic SMTP transport).
It stores one sandboxed table - `messages` (named by a host-opaque `messageId`, carrying opaque `to`/`from` addresses, a host-supplied `transport` adapter tag, a `status` of `queued`/`sending`/`sent`/`failed`, an opaque host `payload`, `attempts`/`maxAttempts` retry bookkeeping, an optional `idempotencyKey`/`subjectRef`/`providerId`/`error`, and `createdAt`/`updatedAt` server timestamps). A host mutation calls `enqueue(messageId, to, from, transport, opts?)` to record the message in `queued` and gets its id back immediately; the host's OWN transport sender then claims it with `markSending`, dispatches it through whatever adapter the host configured (JMAP, an HTTP API, an SMTP-relay shim), and reports the outcome with `markSent(providerId?)` or `markFailed(error?)`; clients poll `get(messageId)` or page `listByStatus(status, paginationOpts)` (reactively in Convex). PROVIDER-NEUTRAL - the component records send intent and status and NEVER calls a mail provider; the transport (Stalwart, Resend, SES, Postmark, an SMTP relay) is host config. `markFailed` re-queues the message while `attempts < maxAttempts`, else lands it in terminal `failed`; the host owns the backoff timing. Terminal states are final - any transition out of `sent`/`failed` is rejected, so a late or duplicate delivery callback never overwrites a recorded outcome. An `idempotencyKey` dedups a re-enqueue (a retry never double-sends). Time is read from the server clock inside every handler - no caller-supplied clock. A bounded, self-rescheduling `prune` (and a built-in daily cron) sweeps terminal messages past a retention window. The host owns the transport, auth, and the `payload` type (the client is generic over it, with an optional `payloadValidator` parser narrowing the opaque stored value at the boundary - never a `v.any()` dump); the component owns only the queue/retry/status core. Mount-safe: it runs correctly under multiple named `app.use` mounts. OPTIONAL GENERIC SMTP TRANSPORT - a separate `@vllnt/convex-email/smtp` export sends a queued message over any SMTP server (Stalwart, Postfix, any provider's relay; the server is host config). The real `nodemailer` send runs in the HOST's own `"use node"` action; the component itself never sends. `nodemailer@^8.0.4` is an OPTIONAL peer dependency (the queue core has zero third-party runtime deps); the floor is the release fixing the `envelope.size` CRLF injection advisory GHSA-c7w3-x93f-qmm8. The adapter is two layers - a PURE, injectable `sendViaSmtp(transport, message, config?)` plus `validateSmtpConfig`/`toMailOptions` (driven by an injected transport, also rejecting CRLF header injection), and a THIN real-`nodemailer` wrapper `createSmtpTransport`/`createSmtpSender`. The host wires the queue to it: page `queued` -> `markSending` -> send -> `markSent(providerId)`/`markFailed(error)`. OPTIONAL GENERIC JMAP TRANSPORT - a second `@vllnt/convex-email/jmap` export sends over any JMAP server's HTTP API (Stalwart, Fastmail, Cyrus, Apache James) from a PLAIN Convex action - JMAP is HTTP, so the host's injected `fetch` runs in V8 with NO `"use node"`, NO `nodemailer`, and zero extra dependencies; it is a protocol not a vendor (`./jmap`, never `./stalwart`). The whole adapter is pure (an injected `fetch`) and 100%-covered: `sendViaJmap(fetchFn, message, config)`, `discoverJmapSession(fetchFn, opts)` (resolves accountId/identityId/Sent-mailbox from the JMAP session), `createJmapSender(config, fetchFn)`, plus the pure builders `validateJmapConfig`/`buildEmailCreate`/`buildSubmitRequest`/`parseSubmitResponse`; it rejects CRLF header injection and sends via the two-call JMAP batch (`Email/set` create -> `EmailSubmission/set` submit). A host can route BOTH transports off one queue by the per-message `transport` tag (`example/convex` `flushQueuedRouted`).

## Examples

- [Example component wrapper](example/convex/example.ts): Host-app wrapper functions
- [Example tests](example/convex/example.test.ts): End-to-end usage coverage with `convex-test`
